footer.brandDesc: The cultural epicenter of the Senegal River valley. A project led by Baaba Maal for the promotion of arts and culture.
footer.newsletterDesc: Receive cultural news from the valley directly in your inbox.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Let's connect
contact.description: A question about the festival, a partnership project, or a request for information about our training programs? The Blues du Fleuve team is here for you.
contact.subject: Subject
contact.successMessage: Thank you for contacting us. Our team will respond as soon as possible.
contact.errorMessage: An error occurred while sending your message. Please try again.
contact.errMessageRequired: Message must be at least 10 characters
contact.faq1Q: How can I participate in the festival as an artist?
contact.faq1A: Send your artistic portfolio (bio, links to your works, contact info) via the form by selecting 'Artist Application'. Our programming team will review your application.
contact.faq2Q: Are partnerships possible with The Village?
contact.faq2A: Yes! The Village welcomes institutional, cultural, and economic partnerships. Select 'Partnership' in the form to get in touch with our dedicated team.
contact.faq3Q: How do I access the training courses offered by NANN-K?
contact.faq3A: The training courses are open to everyone. Go to the Training page or write to us directly. We will provide you with the calendar and registration procedures.
contact.faq4Q: What is the typical response time?
contact.faq4A: Our team commits to responding within 48 business hours. For urgent requests (press, accreditations), please specify it in your message.
contact.seoDesc: Get in touch with the team of The Village Podor and the Les Blues du Fleuve festival in Podor, Senegal. We are at your service for questions, partnerships and training.
contact.seoOgDesc: The Village and Blues du Fleuve festival team welcomes you to Podor. Contact form and coordinates available.
contact.seoKeywords: The Village, contact, Blues du Fleuve, Podor, form, email, phone, Senegal festival

formations.title: Training & Research
formations.subtitle: Learning & Transmission
formations.applyMessage: Thank you for your interest. Our teaching team will review your request and contact you shortly.
formations.heroSubtitle: A center of excellence in the
formations.heroDesc: dedicated to preserving ancestral knowledge and training artists, artisans and researchers from around the world.
formations.centerTitle: Creation of a Training and Research Center
formations.centerDesc1: One of the major pillars of
formations.centerDesc1b: is to rehabilitate in Podor a Research-Action and Training Center on Traditional Music of the Senegal River Valley. Its goal: to document, preserve, teach and promote the musical heritage of the region.
formations.centerDesc2: This educational hub is open to young people from the region eager to embrace their culture, as well as to researchers, ethnomusicologists and international students. It offers a complete program ranging from instrument practice (hoddu, riti, tama, Peul flute) to lutherie, including local crafts.
formations.centerDesc3: Strategic academic partnerships are being developed with UCAD, Gaston Berger University of Saint-Louis and European conservatories.
formations.quoteText: "The music of the river valley is a living heritage. It is our duty to pass it on, to make it grow and to offer it to the whole world."
formations.programsDesc: Six training programs to immerse yourself in the cultural heritage of the valley.
formations.registrationDesc: Ready to develop your talents? Fill out this form to submit your application to one of our programs.
formations.motivationPlaceholder: Briefly explain why you want to join this program...
formations.submitError: An error occurred while submitting your application. Please try again.
formations.adv1: Supervision by recognized master griots and artisans
formations.prog1Desc: Learning and improvement on centuries-old instruments (hoddu, riti, tama, Peul flute), supervised by master griots.
formations.prog2Desc: Transmission of ancestral techniques for making, maintaining and restoring local musical instruments.
formations.prog3Desc: Introduction to ethnomusicology, documentation, transcription and analysis of repertoires, with residencies open to researchers.
formations.prog4Desc: Training in recording and music production techniques at the center's professional studio.
formations.prog5Desc: Practical workshops valuing terracotta work, according to traditional methods of Fouta region artisans.
formations.prog6Desc: Training in traditional soap making techniques and other local products, promoting entrepreneurship and autonomy.

home.heroSubtitle: The cultural epicenter of the river valley. A unique center regrouping museum, creation and training spaces.
home.wuroPodor: Wuro Podor, nde fii ndu jom
home.introText1: The Village is much more than a cultural center.
home.introText2: It is an authentic village in the heart of Podor.
home.introText3: Dedicated to the preservation of the Haalpulaar culture.
home.introText4: And the musical heritage of the Senegal River.
home.introText6: It offers the youth of Fouta a place.
home.introText8: A living space to create, learn, share and celebrate.
home.activityMusicDesc: At the heart of the village, recording studios, rehearsal rooms and spaces dedicated to the practice of traditional instruments.
home.activityTrainingDesc: Music, crafts, pottery and soap making workshops to transmit ancestral know-how to young generations.
home.activityEventsDesc: Concerts, exhibitions, conferences and festivals that make The Village a dynamic and essential cultural place in the region.
home.bioShort: "The Village is the culmination of a dream: to offer the youth of Podor a place where tradition and modernity meet to create the future."
home.activitiesTitle: The Cultural Life of The Village
home.activityConcertsDesc: Experience unforgettable moments with live concerts, performances by international and local artists, in a magical setting on the banks of the Senegal River.
home.activityExhibitionsDesc: Discover contemporary and traditional art exhibitions, highlighting the Haalpulaar heritage and local know-how.
home.activityConferencesDesc: Discussions on sustainable development, culture, education and the future of Africa with experts and local actors.
home.activityMasterclassDesc: Learn from masters: music, dance, crafts workshops, transmission of ancestral know-how.
home.activityFashionDesc: Highlight Senegalese and African designers with traditional and contemporary fashion shows.
home.newsletterDesc: Receive cultural news and analyses of the NANN-k project.
home.newsletterError: Unable to register your email. Please try again.

tickets.title: Official Ticketing
tickets.subtitle: Get your passes
tickets.notifySuccess: You'll be notified when it opens!
tickets.heroDesc: Get ready to live an unforgettable experience in the heart of Fouta Toro. Book your official passes for the Blues du Fleuve festival now.
tickets.notifyDesc: Sign up to receive the latest news and exclusive invitations by email.
tickets.passDayDesc: Access to one full day of the festival
tickets.passDayFeat1: Access to the festival site (The Village)
tickets.passDayFeat2: Access to all concerts of the day
tickets.pass3DaysDesc: The full Blues du Fleuve experience
tickets.pass3DaysFeat1: Full access to all 3 days of the festival
tickets.passVIPDesc: Experience the festival in exclusive conditions
tickets.passVIPFeat2: Access to VIP area with stunning view
tickets.faq1A: We accept online payments by bank card (Visa, Mastercard), Orange Money, Wave and Free Money.
tickets.faq2A: Tickets are not refundable, except in case of festival cancellation by us. They can however be transferred to another person.
tickets.faq3A: Access is free for children under 12 accompanied by an adult with a valid ticket.
tickets.faq4Q: How do I get my ticket?
tickets.faq4A: After your simulated purchase, you can immediately download your e-ticket in SVG format or keep it on screen. It contains a QR code to validate your entry.
tickets.securityMessage: Transactions are simulated and secure. Your personal information remains confidential.

tv.title: NANN-k
tv.subtitle: Documentaries, live concerts and preservation of the river valley traditions.
tv.featuredTitle: Podor, living memory of the river

festival.heroDesc1: Baptized by the international Senegalese artist
festival.heroDesc1b: , the Blues du Fleuve festival is celebrating its 17th edition.
festival.heroDesc2: The only integration festival in West Africa, the Blues du Fleuve symbolizes the solidarity of the countries bordering the Senegal River.
festival.heroLongDesc1: The Festival dedicates its editions through various expressions of peoples whose culture is strongly influenced by water. This culture is represented mainly by the performing arts: music, dance, traditional shows and crafts. This popular heritage and development concerns are also represented through thematic exhibitions and conferences.
festival.heroLongDesc2: This event will be inscribed this year under the sign of peace and social harmony for African living together in unity and diversity, on the one hand, and on the emergence of Senegal, on the other.
festival.themeSubtitle: "Culture – Security – Environment and Solidarity around the Senegal River"
festival.presentationTitle1: The Festival and the City of
festival.presentationDesc: A framework of excellence for African culture, organized at the initiative of
festival.obj1: Unite the peoples of the Senegal River
festival.producerMsg1: Yes, the Blues du Fleuve Festival has become a breath, a driving force, a glorious moment where Podor lives and smiles more. Holding a festival every year is a major challenge.
festival.producerMsg2: Dear festival-goers from Senegal, neighboring countries, the rest of Africa and abroad, you are at home here, on this land where the river carries our history, our culture and our ancestral traditions.
festival.producerMsg3: Welcome to Podor Wuro Njaak Buubu and Baaba Maal, where music unites us and brings us together; where Baaba's warm voice delights our hearts and awakens our spirits.
festival.producerQuote: I wish you a magnificent festival.
festival.programDesc: Three days of music, culture and celebration in the heart of Podor
festival.day3Event3: Closing Concert (Baaba Maal & Le Daande Lenol)
festival.artistsSubtitle: Voices and talents of the river.
festival.gallerySubtitle: A visual immersion in the heart of the festival
festival.mediaArchivesSubtitle: Relive the highlights of the festival
festival.contactDesc: We are at your disposal. Our back office will handle your requests.
festival.pillar1Desc: Music and traditional dances representative of the richness of Halpulaar and Toucouleur.
festival.pillar2Desc: Exhibition of local know-how and traditional art objects of the valley.
festival.pillar3Desc: Development panels and thematic reflections on the concerns of populations.

nannk.heroDescFull: Ndemma (agriculture) · Awo (Fishing) · Ngaynaka (Livestock) · Nalankagal (Culture - serving as a development engine) · Karalagal (New technologies, to modernize the other sectors).
nannk.foundationsDesc: A social and economic emergence movement structured around 5 strategic activity sectors:
nannk.pillar1Desc: Guarantee food security, enhance terroirs and make agriculture a lever for inclusive growth.
nannk.pillar2Desc: Enhance the fishery resources of the Senegal River and support artisanal fishing communities.
nannk.pillar3Desc: Develop traditional pastoral livestock farming, optimize herd management and modernize the sector.
nannk.pillar4Desc: Serve as a cultural and social engine for education, identity and transmission in the valley.
nannk.pillar5Desc: Modernize all traditional activity sectors through innovative digital tools.
nannk.missionDesc1: NANN-K is a citizen movement that instills new dynamics in the heart of the Senegal River valley. It promotes five essential action axes: the
nannk.missionDesc2: Our credo? "Culture – Knowledge – Work". We are building an active citizen consciousness so that every inhabitant of the valley becomes an actor of their own future.
nannk.why1Text: Africa faces colossal challenges. Institutional initiatives do not always take root in local realities. NANN-K was born from this observation: change must come from citizens themselves.
nannk.why2Text: Jobs, income, food security: needs are crying out. NANN-K provides the tools: training, access to financing, equipment and strong media visibility.
nannk.why3Text: Senegal has made agriculture its growth engine. NANN-K is the concrete alternative: on-site support, close to farmers.
nannk.tvDesc1: is the audiovisual channel of The Village – voice of the musical heritage of the Senegal River valley. Documentaries, live concerts, cultural programs, portraits of griots and masterclasses of traditional instruments.
nannk.tvDesc2: Available on all digital platforms, NANN-k TV aims to be the living and broadcast memory of Fouta Toro – accessible from Podor as from Paris, New York or Dakar.
nannk.trustTitle: NANNK TRUST supports the fight against desertification
nannk.trustDesc1: The NANNK TRUST foundation, initiated by the global artist Baaba Maal, has handed over a check of more than
nannk.trustDesc1c: , marking a key step in their partnership for the environment.
nannk.trustDesc2: This contribution constitutes the second tranche of the financing committed by the foundation to support reforestation and restoration of local ecosystems.
nannk.trustQuote: Protecting the environment is an urgency and a collective responsibility. By supporting Podor Vert, we are investing in a sustainable future for our communities and for future generations.
nannk.reforestationDesc1: As part of the national tree day initiated by President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, the Podor department manifested itself through Deputy Ismaela Wone and the Podor Vert association with a major reforestation day in the commune of
nannk.reforestationDesc2: The villages of Lougué Sebbé, Lougué Toroobé and Lougué Fulbé benefited from
nannk.reforestationDesc2b: from the nurseries of Fanaye and Mery.
nannk.reforestationCTA: Together, let's work for a green and sustainable Podor.
nannk.agriSpaceDesc: The NANN-K agricultural space is the heart of our agriculture pillar. It serves as a model for sustainable farming, integrating modern irrigation and cultivation techniques to maximize yields while preserving the resources of the Senegal River valley.
nannk.joinDesc: Whether you are a local actor, a potential partner or simply passionate about the development of the valley, your voice counts.
nannk.browserNoVideo: Your browser does not support video.
nannk.seoTitle: NANN-k & The Village | Podor Cultural & Economic Movement
nannk.seoDesc: Discover NANN-k and The Village, Baaba Maal's initiative for culture, agriculture and development in the Senegal River Valley.
nannk.seoOgTitle: NANN-k & The Village - Baaba Maal Podor
nannk.seoOgDesc: NANN-k and The Village: citizen movement for social and economic emergence through agriculture, crafts and Halpulaar culture.
nannk.seoKeywords: The Village, NANN-k, Baaba Maal, agriculture, crafts, technologies, development, Senegal, Africa, Podor, economic emergence
